entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A highly nutritious and resilient cereal crop whose flour has a short shelf-life due to rapid rancidity development

entities.name: RANCIDITY
entities.description: A complex and quantitative trait in pearl millet that leads to short shelf-life of flour

entities.name: METABOLOMICS
entities.description: A method used to identify differential accumulation of metabolites to understand the biochemical mechanisms of rancidity

entities.name: LC-HRMS
entities.description: Liquid chromatography and high-resolution mass spectrometry used for metabolite profiling in pearl millet genotypes

entities.name: ACCELERATED AGEING CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental conditions used to induce rancidity for the purpose of metabolite profiling

entities.name: FATTY ACIDS
entities.description: Biochemical pathways and metabolites associated with the rancidity process in pearl millet

entities.name: GLYCEROPHOSPHOLIPIDS
entities.description: Biochemical pathways identified through non-targeted metabolomic analysis as associated with rancidity

entities.name: SPHINGOLIPIDS
entities.description: Biochemical pathways identified through non-targeted metabolomic analysis as associated with rancidity

entities.name: TERPENOIDS
entities.description: Biochemical pathways identified through non-targeted metabolomic analysis as associated with rancidity

entities.name: FATTY ALDEHYDES
entities.description: Metabolites significantly elevated in high rancid genotypes of pearl millet

entities.description: Breakdown products of fatty acids-related metabolites involved in rancidity

entities.description: Breakdown products of fatty acids-related metabolites involved in rancidity

entities.name: STEARIC ACID
entities.description: A key fatty acid quantified in pearl millet genotypes that confirms its role in rancidity development

entities.description: A method to develop low-rancidity pearl millet lines facilitated by metabolomics insights
